item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some spicy reading +1 all day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The thing about the insects developing complex societies kind of reminds me of the "Gi-Ants" episode from my favorite TV show, Phineas and Ferb, where ants develop complex societies, starting with farming, then moving on to an "Industrial Revolution", forming a democracy, and entering the "Information Age", and eventually going into space! +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconded. The extermination after every test is key to this skip, but the article needs to justify that naturally and implicitly. The logs are actually pretty close already. If the page ended right after the redacted 3-day test I would definitely upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like it, reminds me of 1006.. in a lot of ways actually.. even has a dead guy..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No immediate complaints from me (except the fact that I wanted to see a bug war), but I find this article a little similar to 1535 and for that I'm upvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rather than termination on the assumption that this is gonna bite them in the ass, maybe give an example of an incident that does just that.

The risk of having something like this occur is that you might paint Foundation personnel as dangerously irresponsible. I feel like the Foundation has a mindset of 'there's not such thing as too careful'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can only speak for myself here, and not the others who downvoted, but I would prefer a lot more "showing" and not "telling". Dr. Helm brings up the concern that the insects will advance themselves too quickly, which is a valid concern, but maybe we could actually get an example of that happening? Rather than termination on the assumption that this is gonna bite them in the ass, maybe give an example of an incident that does just that. At the end of the day, they are still just insects, and unless they end up escaping (which in itself is an interesting idea) they can't really do much harm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey Toa, sorry you didn't enjoy it. The note from Dr. Helm was actually an addition that was made later during writing at the suggestion that I address the ethical concerns of the SCP. If I were to attempt this again at a later date, is there anything else I might do to improve it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm going to no vote, because the concept is interesting and the SCP is competently written. But that last note tanks it for me. It feels as subtle as a sledgehammer.
